title: Dense Smoke Hot Filipino Challots Menudo
description: A hearty, smoky Filipino dish featuring challots, perfect for a post-workout meal. This menudo-style recipe is shojin-friendly and packed with flavor.
instructions: Heat the coconut oil in a large pot over medium heat.
Add the sliced challots and cook until they are translucent and starting to caramelize.
Add the minced garlic and grated ginger and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the smoked paprika, tomato paste, and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Pour in the vegetable broth and bring the mixture to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer the menudo for 20 minutes, or until the flavors have melded together and the challots are tender.
Season the menudo with salt and black pepper to taste.
Add the dried bay leaves and simmer for an additional 5 minutes.
Remove the bay leaves and serve the menudo hot.
